summary: REQ-1 appears implemented via updated remaining-time formatting without the word “left”. REQ-2 appears largely implemented by introducing an immediate in-form status/progress panel with explicit “preparing/uploading/creating” stages, but the diff summary does not clearly show the submit button being disabled throughout. REQ-3 is partially addressed with faster time-to-first-feedback (“preparing” shown before file reading) and progress callbacks, but there’s insufficient evidence of changes that minimize time-to-first-upload-progress or materially improve upload throughput beyond UI feedback.

missing_requirements.requirement: The “Create Paste” button remains disabled during the entire operation to prevent duplicate submissions, and the status panel is the primary indicator (not a delayed secondary indicator).
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows guards preventing file changes/removal during upload, and it shows an immediate status panel, but it does not explicitly show the “Create Paste” button being disabled during the whole mutation lifecycle (JSX portion is truncated).

missing_requirements.requirement: Minimize time-to-first-upload-progress event and improve overall upload performance where possible (not just time-to-first-user-feedback), without changing the 50MB per-file limit behavior.
missing_requirements.reason: The changes clearly improve time-to-first-user-feedback via an immediate “preparing” stage and a forced render tick, but file upload progress still appears to start only after reading the entire file into memory (await file.arrayBuffer()) before saveFile begins. No clear diff evidence shows a restructuring that would start byte-level upload earlier or reduce total upload time.

hallucinated_features.description: Added/changed user profile APIs and related authorization checks (getCallerUserProfile, getUserProfile, saveCallerUserProfile) in the backend.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by any requirement; requirements focus on expiration wording and upload/progress UX/performance.

hallucinated_features.description: Paste ID validation/normalization utilities and routing behavior for extracting/normalizing paste IDs (handling trailing slashes, query strings, URL decoding).
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested in REQ-1/2/3; appears to be an additional robustness feature.

hallucinated_features.description: Global design/theme changes (warm neutral OKLCH palette) and Tailwind configuration extensions.
hallucinated_features.reason: UI theme/palette changes were not requested; requirements were specific to expiration wording and upload progress behavior.

hallucinated_features.description: New/expanded file download utility with MIME-to-extension mapping and delayed Object URL cleanup to improve mobile filename behavior.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested; outside the scope of expiration wording and upload progress UX/performance.
